page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower-to-average tiers. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng falls within the bottom 25%, while Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests achieve average ratings. Memory performance is mixed, with read-only bandwidth benchmarks scoring in the bottom 25%, whereas large-block write bandwidth reaches average levels. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression, show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 10%.

From a workload perspective, the instance is suited for general-purpose tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high-performance computing or massive memory bandwidth. Its balanced resource allocation makes it appropriate for moderate web serving, basic database operations, and lightweight application hosting. However, the lack of local storage and lower benchmark results for intensive tasks like text processing and large-model LLM inference suggest it is less optimal for high-throughput data processing or real-time analytics.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate moderate performance, with Passmark CPU scores in the average tier, while memory read bandwidth and single-core stress-ng tests fall into the bottom 25%. This instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including moderate web serving, basic database hosting, and lightweight application environments that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high-performance computing or high-speed local storage.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core general-purpose instance with balanced memory allocation for standard application hosting and moderate web serving workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
